title: Noise Sources in Quantum Hardware: A Comprehensive Overview
description: Master the fundamental physics of noise in quantum computing systems. Learn to identify, characterize, and model the diverse noise sources that limit qubit coherence and gate fidelity across different hardware platforms.
learningObjectives: Classify quantum noise sources by their physical origin and spectral characteristics
Derive the relationship between noise power spectral density and decoherence rates
Apply the spin-boson model to understand qubit-environment coupling
Distinguish between Markovian and non-Markovian noise processes
Calculate decoherence rates from environmental noise spectra
